name: BTLM-3B-8K
archDisplay: Dense transformer, GPT-2 style + SwiGLU + ALiBi + muP (32 layers, d_model 2560)
note: Bittensor Language Model: 2.6B (marketed as 3B) with 7B-class quality and 8K context, trained on 627B tokens of SlimPajama, trained on the Condor Galaxy 1 (CG-1) supercomputer (Cerebras/G42 partnership).
tpsNote: Cerebras reports the model fits in under 3GB of memory at 4-bit precision for mobile/edge deployment.

results.notes: 5-shot, Table 5. Sits slightly below the taxonomy's stated 30-83 range floor for this slug -- reflects BTLM being one of the smallest models in the corpus, not a data error

verificationNotes: CORRECTED releaseDate: input record cited 2023-09-20 (the arXiv paper's submission date), but the model was actually publicly released earlier -- HuggingFace commit history shows weights uploaded ~2023-07-24, and Cerebras/Opentensor's own announcement says BTLM 'went live on Bittensor' July 27, 2023; downgraded to month precision (2023-07) since sources disagree on the exact day. Confirmed exact param count is 2.6B (not marketed '3B') via the paper. All 5 result rows (hellaswag 69.8, arc-challenge 37.6, winogrande 65.8, mmlu 28.1, humaneval 9.9) verified as literally present in Table 2 of the paper via the ar5iv HTML rendering (a text-accurate mirror of the arXiv LaTeX source) -- notably an initial web-search-synthesized read of the same paper produced shuffled/wrong numbers (e.g. arc-challenge misread as 65.8), which the direct ar5iv HTML fetch corrected. Apache-2.0 and 8192 context confirmed via HF model card.
